note: Lines 84-93 in DB have words severely out of order. Words 'supervisory', 'necessarily', 'create extraordinary', 'circumstances', 'justifying' are split across separate lines in wrong sequence. PDF shows the correct phrase on contiguous lines: 'does not\nnecessarily create extraordinary circumstances justifying supervisory\njurisdiction.' This is a major OCR/ingestion error where words were reordered and fragmented.

The phrase 'in Nichols was to nullify California's state' appears orphaned at the end of paragraph [¶17], when it should be part of 'The plaintiff's goal in Nichols was to nullify California's state implementation plan (SIP).' in paragraph [¶18]. This is a major ingestion/OCR defect where text was split across paragraph boundaries incorrectly.

old_exact: The term in conduct "intentionally, knowingly, or "willfully" means to engage recklessly."
new_exact: The term "willfully" means to engage in conduct "intentionally, knowingly, or recklessly."
source: Comparison with PDF source /Users/jerod/refs/nd/opin/pdfs/2025/2025ND143.pdf line ~171-172; statute cited N.D.C.C. § 12.1-02-02(1)(e) defines willfully as engaging 'in conduct' with enumerated mental states
note: OCR/ingestion error: the phrase 'in conduct' was moved to the beginning and the elements of the definition were scrambled. The correct statutory reading is 'The term "willfully" means to engage in conduct "intentionally, knowingly, or recklessly."' per the PDF source.

note: Critical OCR/ingestion defect in paragraph [¶54]. The statute quote text was scrambled during PDF-to-text conversion. The phrase 'improvements caused by drilling operations.' The term' was incorrectly split, losing 'value of' at the critical point and duplicating text ('In improvements caused by drilling operations.' The granting' should be 'In granting'). The correct complete statute quote reads: '...for . . . lost value of improvements caused by drilling operations." The term "improvements" is not defined...' Both old and new are exactly 421 characters; verified count = 1 (byte-exact match in DB file, single unique occurrence).

evidence: Line 27 contains Greek capital letter Iota (U+0399) instead of Latin capital I (U+0041). Verified by rendered PDF showing simple section header 'I' as a Roman numeral section divider.

Line 71 contains LaTeX markup '$\mathsf{C}$' instead of plain Latin letter C. This is a section header that should be a simple letter, not mathematical markup. Confirmed by rendered PDF showing simple letter 'C' as section divider.
Line 97 contains Cyrillic capital B (U+0412) instead of Latin capital B (U+0042). Verified by rendered PDF showing simple section header 'B' as Roman numeral section marker.

Line 117 contains LaTeX markup '$\mathsf{C}$' instead of plain Latin letter C. Second occurrence of same OCR error at different location. Section header should be simple letter, not mathematical notation.

note: ¶15 ellipsis: DB shows spaced four-dot form '[ . . . ]' but PDF source (pdftotext) shows compact three-dot form '[...]'. Per ND court style, ellipses in opinions use the compact form. This occurs in context 'N.D.R.Juv.P. 10: (a) Presence. [...] (3) Parent, Guardian...' where bracketed ellipsis indicates omitted rule text.

new_exact: falls within the plain description of post-production costs to "include[e] transportation, gathering, compression, processing, treating, and marketing costs[]" that the lessee incur[s] after extracting the oil or gas from the ground.
